title: alnus glutinosa var. laciniata
intro: Alnus glutinosa var. laciniata, also known as the cut-leaved alder, is a variety of the common alder tree (Alnus glutinosa) native to Europe. The medicinal properties of the plant lie in its bark, leaves, and cones, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Alnus glutinosa var. laciniata include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Alnus glutinosa var. laciniata is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its medicinal properties can be extracted through tinctures and decoctions, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
